notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Read-only window with queued publish-later work
summary: A read-only window queues a managed publish for later and saves a provider publish as a local draft.

affected_scope.scope_summary: Managed-cloud and self-hosted review publishing in the AP-south region.

boundary_change.summary: No tenant, region, or endpoint boundary change.

blocked_writes.note: Review comments queue for publish-later with an idempotency key and replay when the window ends.
A provider publish is saved as a local draft and survives the read-only window.

succeeded_hosted_mutations.note: An approval landed before the window and is listed separately from queued work.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local search continue.
Local Git commit and branch continue.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local-first work continues; queued and local-draft publishes survive the window.

display_copy.primary_status_line: Read-only window: managed writes queue for later or save as local drafts; reads continue.

display_copy.queued_preserved_line: 2 intents preserved (1 publish-later queued, 1 local drafts); they survive the window.

display_copy.local_continuity_line: Local-first work continues; queued and local-draft publishes survive the window.
display_copy.boundary_change_line: No tenant / region / endpoint boundary change.
display_copy.follow_up_line: Queued and local-draft work will replay when the window resumes.
